title: ilex macrocarpa var. reevesiae
intro: Ilex macrocarpa var. reevesiae, also known as Reeves' Holly, is a variety of the Ilex macrocarpa plant, a species of holly native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ilex macrocarpa var. reevesiae include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
